en.extract:
The Kidepo lark (Corypha kidepoensis) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae found in South Sudan and Uganda. It was formerly treated as a subspecies of the red-winged lark.


Taxonomy
The Kidepo lark was formally described in 1940 by the Scottish-Australian ornithologist James Macdonald based on a specimen that he had collected in southern South Sudan at Ero on the boundary of Didinga Hills with the plain of the Kidepo River. He considered it as a subspecies of the red-winged lark and coined the trinomial name Mirafra hypermetra kidepoensis. The Kidepo lark is now treated as a separate species and placed in the genus Corypha based on the results of molecular phylogenetic studies by a team of ornithologists led by Per Alström that were published in 2023 and 2024.
Two subspecies are recognised:

C. k. kathangorensis (Cave, 1940) – southeast South Sudan
C. k. kidepoensis (Macdonald, 1940) – south South Sudan and northeast Uganda

The '''Kidepo lark''' ('''''Corypha kidepoensis''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]] found in South Sudan and Uganda. It was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the [[red-winged lark]].

==Taxonomy==
The Kidepo lark was [[species description|formally described]] in 1940 by the Scottish-Australian ornithologist [[James Macdonald (ornithologist)|James Macdonald]] based on a specimen that he had collected in southern South Sudan at Ero on the boundary of [[Didinga Hills]] with the plain of the [[Kidepo River]]. He considered it as a [[subspecies]] of the [[red-winged lark]] and coined the [[trinomial name]] ''Mirafra hypermetra kidepoensis''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Macdonald | first=James David | author-link=James Macdonald (ornithologist) | year=1940 | title=A new race of francolin and a new race of lark from the Sudan | journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club | volume=60 | pages=57-59 [59] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/40512218 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology  | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts  | page=9 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480684 }}</ref> The Kidepo lark is now treated as a separate species and placed in the genus ''[[Corypha (bird)|Corypha]]'' based on the results of [[molecular phylogenetic]] studies by a team of ornithologists led by [[Per Alström]] that were published in 2023 and 2024.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Alström | first1=P. | author1-link=Per Alström | using multilocus and genomic data | journal=Avian Research | volume=14 | article-number=100095 | doi=10.1016/j.avrs.2023.100095 | doi-access=free| hdl=11370/bca6ef93-5fe0-40f0-8354-558e15c425fd | Two subspecies are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''C. k. kathangorensis'' (Cave, 1940) – southeast South Sudan
* ''C. k. kidepoensis'' ([[James Macdonald (ornithologist)|Macdonald]], 1940) – south South Sudan and northeast Uganda
